req.title: Hr-Admin creates policy-document
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to create a policy-document through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) happy-path creation by an authenticated hr-admin with all mandatory fields supplied (EP – valid partition); (2) submission with a mandatory field empty, expecting a 400 validation error (NEG – invalid input); (3) title field at its minimum acceptable length boundary of 1 character (BVA); (4) unauthenticated access returning 401 (NEG – auth state; heuristic corrected from STC to NEG because the test exercises a negative/error pathway, not a state-transition sequence); (5) a forbidden role (employee) receiving 403 (DT – role-based decision table). Together these cover positive, negative, and boundary kinds with full error-pathway traceability. Fix applied: test[3] kind=negative heuristic changed from STC to NEG to resolve the logical violation.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_creates_policy_document_all_fields_valid
preconditions: No policy-document with the same title exists
actions: POST /policy-documents with all mandatory fields populated with valid representative values
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains a non-empty entity id
An audit record exists for the created policy-document
trace: R-WEB-053: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_hr_admin_creates_policy_document_all_fields_valid(
    hr_admin_client,        # <fixture> returns authenticated requests.Session
    audit_log_helper,       # <fixture> wraps audit-log query API
    cleanup_policy_doc,     # <fixture> deletes doc by id after test
    base_url,               # <fixture> e.g. "https://hr-app.internal"
):
    payload = {
        "title": "Annual Leave Policy",
        "content": "Employees are entitled to 20 days annual leave.",
        "effective_date": "2025-01-01",
        "category": "leave",
    }
    resp = hr_admin_client.post(f"{base_url}/policy-documents", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"

    body = resp.json()
    entity_id = body.get("id") or body.get("entity_id")
    assert entity_id, "Response must contain a non-empty entity id"

    cleanup_policy_doc(entity_id)

    audit_exists = audit_log_helper.record_exists(
        action="CREATE",
        resource="policy-document",
        resource_id=entity_id,
    )
    assert audit_exists, "Audit record must be written on successful creation"

tests.name: test_create_policy_document_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains a validation error message referencing the missing field
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_create_policy_document_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400(
    hr_admin_client,
    base_url,
):
    payload = {
        # 'title' intentionally omitted to trigger mandatory-field validation
        "content": "Some policy content.",
        "effective_date": "2025-01-01",
        "category": "leave",
    }
    resp = hr_admin_client.post(f"{base_url}/policy-documents", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 400, f"Expected 400, got {resp.status_code}"

    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "title" in error_text, (
        "Validation error must reference the missing 'title' field"
    )

tests.name: test_create_policy_document_title_minimum_length_boundary
preconditions: Minimum allowed title length is 1 character
actions: POST /policy-documents with title set to exactly 1 character
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains a non-empty entity id
trace: R-WEB-053: mandatory fields must be validated
R-WEB-053: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_create_policy_document_title_minimum_length_boundary(
    hr_admin_client,
    cleanup_policy_doc,
    base_url,
):
    payload = {
        "title": "A",  # exactly 1 character – lower boundary value
        "content": "Minimal title boundary test.",
        "effective_date": "2025-06-01",
        "category": "general",
    }
    resp = hr_admin_client.post(f"{base_url}/policy-documents", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Single-char title should be accepted; got {resp.status_code}"
    )

    body = resp.json()
    entity_id = body.get("id") or body.get("entity_id")
    assert entity_id, "Response must contain a non-empty entity id for min-length title"

    cleanup_policy_doc(entity_id)

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_request_to_create_policy_document_returns_401
preconditions: No authentication token is present (unauthenticated state)

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_cannot_create_policy_document_returns_403
preconditions: User is authenticated with role 'employee' (not hr-admin)
actions: POST /policy-documents using an employee-role session with all mandatory fields populated
